maml:description.maml:para: Builds a filter expression for Edwin SDT schedules.
Build-EAISdtFilter interactively creates a filter condition object for use with New-EAISdt, Set-EAISdt, and other Edwin Action Service cmdlets.
The completed filter is saved to the global `$EAIFilter` variable.
Optional current filter to keep or replace when updating a schedule.
Returns the filter object instead of only saving it to `$EAIFilter`.
Optional current filter to keep or replace when updating a schedule.
Returns the filter object instead of only saving it to `$EAIFilter`.

dev:type.dev:name: None. You cannot pipe objects to this command.
Returns an Edwin filter object when using -PassThru.

maml:alert.maml:para: Use Connect-EAIAccount before running Edwin cmdlets that submit the filter. Requires sdt_read scope to load field metadata from the API when available.

maml:introduction.maml:para: Build-EAISdtFilter
$filter = Build-EAISdtFilter -PassThru New-EAISdt -Name 'Maintenance' -Duration 60 -Filter $filter
Build-EAISdtFilter -ExistingFilter (Get-EAISdt -Id $id).filter

maml:description.maml:para: Builds a filter expression for Logic Monitor API queries.

The Build-LMFilter function creates a filter expression by interactively prompting for conditions and operators. It supports basic filtering for single fields and advanced filtering for property-based queries. Multiple conditions can be combined using AND/OR operators. When a ResourcePath is provided, the wizard validates field names and provides suggestions.
When specified, returns the filter expression as a string instead of displaying it in a panel.
Optional. The API endpoint path (e.g., '/device/devices') to provide field validation and suggestions.
When specified, returns the filter expression as a string instead of displaying it in a panel.
Optional. The API endpoint path (e.g., '/device/devices') to provide field validation and suggestions.

dev:type.dev:name: None. You cannot pipe objects to this command.

maml:alert.maml:para: The filter expression is saved to the global $LMFilter variable. When ResourcePath is provided, field names are validated against the API schema.

maml:introduction.maml:para: #Build a basic filter expression Build-LMFilter This example launches the interactive filter builder wizard.
#Build a filter with field validation for devices Build-LMFilter -ResourcePath "/device/devices" This example launches the wizard with field validation and suggestions for the device endpoint.
#Build a filter and return the expression Build-LMFilter -PassThru This example builds a filter and returns the expression as a string.

maml:description.maml:para: Connect to an Edwin portal for event ingestion.

Connect-EAIAccount establishes a session for sending custom third-party events to Edwin. This is separate from Connect-LMAccount. LogicMonitor alerts are already sent to Edwin natively; use these cmdlets to ingest events from external systems such as Meraki, ServiceNow, or custom applications.
When credential validation is enabled, Connect-EAIAccount exchanges client credentials for a Bearer access token via POST /auth/token.
The client ID used for Edwin OAuth2 client credentials authentication.
The client secret used for Edwin OAuth2 client credentials authentication.
Disables informational messages for subsequent commands. Console logging is enabled by default.
The Edwin organization subdomain (the name before ".dexda.ai").
Skip local validation of required credential fields and the remote token grant check.
Path to a YAML auth file containing edwin_org, client_id, and client_secret.
Disables informational messages for subsequent commands. Console logging is enabled by default.
Skip local validation of required credential fields and the remote token grant check.
The cached Edwin account name to use from the Logic.Monitor secret vault.
Disables informational messages for subsequent commands. Console logging is enabled by default.
Skip local validation of required credential fields and the remote token grant check.
Load credentials from the Logic.Monitor secret vault using interactive selection.
Path to a YAML auth file containing edwin_org, client_id, and client_secret.
The cached Edwin account name to use from the Logic.Monitor secret vault.
The client ID used for Edwin OAuth2 client credentials authentication.
The client secret used for Edwin OAuth2 client credentials authentication.
Disables informational messages for subsequent commands. Console logging is enabled by default.
The Edwin organization subdomain (the name before ".dexda.ai").
Skip local validation of required credential fields and the remote token grant check.
Load credentials from the Logic.Monitor secret vault using interactive selection.

dev:type.dev:name: None.

maml:introduction.maml:para: Connect-EAIAccount -EdwinOrg "myorg" -ClientId "client-id" -ClientSecret "client-secret"
Connect-EAIAccount -CachedAccountName "EAI:myorg"

maml:introduction.maml:para: ``` #Connecting to an Account using an Access ID and Access Key Connect-LMAccount -AccessId xxxxxx -AccessKey xxxxxx -AccountName subdomain ```
``` #Connecting to an Account using a Bearer Token Connect-LMAccount -BearerToken xxxxxx -AccountName subdomain ```
``` #Connecting to an Account using a Cached Credential Connect-LMAccount -UseCachedCredential -CachedAccountName "CachedAccountName" ```

maml:description.maml:para: Converts third-party objects into Edwin CEF event payloads.
Convert-EAIEvent maps arbitrary input objects to Edwin CEF payloads using a property map. Map values can be source property names or scriptblocks for computed values.
Optional default event_source value when not mapped.
The third-party object to convert.
Hashtable mapping CEF field names to source property names or scriptblocks.
Optional hashtable mapping vendor severity aliases to Edwin severity names or values.
Optional default event_source value when not mapped.
The third-party object to convert.
Hashtable mapping CEF field names to source property names or scriptblocks.
Optional hashtable mapping vendor severity aliases to Edwin severity names or values.

dev:type.dev:name: System.Object
PSCustomObject with cef and enrichments properties.

maml:description.maml:para: Migrates LogicMonitor website checks to LM Uptime devices.

ConvertTo-LMUptimeDevice consumes objects returned by Get-LMWebsite, translates their configuration into the v3 Uptime payload shape, and provisions new Uptime devices by invoking New-LMUptimeDevice. The cmdlet preserves alerting behaviour, polling thresholds, locations, and scripted web steps whenever possible.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
When specified, disables alerting on the source website after the Uptime device is created successfully.
Optional string prefixed to the generated Uptime device name.
Optional string appended to the generated Uptime device name.
Explicit host group identifiers for the new device.
Website object returned by Get-LMWebsite. Accepts pipeline input.
Runs the command in a mode that only reports what would happen without performing the actions.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
When specified, disables alerting on the source website after the Uptime device is created successfully.
Optional string prefixed to the generated Uptime device name.
Optional string appended to the generated Uptime device name.
Explicit host group identifiers for the new device.
Website object returned by Get-LMWebsite. Accepts pipeline input.
Runs the command in a mode that only reports what would happen without performing the actions.

dev:type.dev:name: PSObject. Website objects returned by Get-LMWebsite can be piped to this cmdlet.
System.Management.Automation.PSObject
LogicMonitor.LMUptimeDevice

maml:alert.maml:para: You must run Connect-LMAccount prior to execution. The cmdlet honours -WhatIf/-Confirm through ShouldProcess.

maml:introduction.maml:para: Get-LMWebsite -Name "logicmonitor.com" | ConvertTo-LMUptimeDevice -NameSuffix "-uptime"
Migrates the logicmonitor.com website check to an Uptime device with a "-uptime" suffix.

maml:description.maml:para: Copies a LogicMonitor dashboard to a new dashboard.

The Copy-LMDashboard function allows you to copy a LogicMonitor dashboard to a new dashboard. You can specify the name, ID, or group name of the dashboard to be copied, as well as provide a new name and optional description for the copied dashboard. The function requires valid API credentials and a logged-in session.
The ID of the dashboard to be copied. This parameter is mandatory when using the 'GroupId-Id' or 'GroupName-Id' parameter sets.
A hashtable of tokens to be replaced in the dashboard. The key is the token name and the value is the new value. If not provided, the tokens from the source dashboard will be used.
An optional description for the new dashboard.
The name of the new dashboard.
The name of the parent group for the new dashboard. This parameter is mandatory when using the 'GroupName-Id' or 'GroupName-Name' parameter sets.
The ID of the dashboard to be copied. This parameter is mandatory when using the 'GroupId-Id' or 'GroupName-Id' parameter sets.

An optional description for the new dashboard.
The name of the new dashboard.
The ID of the parent group for the new dashboard. This parameter is mandatory when using the 'GroupId-Id' or 'GroupId-Name' parameter sets.
The name of the dashboard to be copied. This parameter is mandatory when using the 'GroupId-Name' or 'GroupName-Name' parameter sets.
A hashtable of tokens to be replaced in the dashboard. The key is the token name and the value is the new value. If not provided, the tokens from the source dashboard will be used.
An optional description for the new dashboard.
The name of the new dashboard.
The name of the parent group for the new dashboard. This parameter is mandatory when using the 'GroupName-Id' or 'GroupName-Name' parameter sets.
The name of the dashboard to be copied. This parameter is mandatory when using the 'GroupId-Name' or 'GroupName-Name' parameter sets.
A hashtable of tokens to be replaced in the dashboard. The key is the token name and the value is the new value. If not provided, the tokens from the source dashboard will be used.
An optional description for the new dashboard.
The name of the new dashboard.
The ID of the parent group for the new dashboard. This parameter is mandatory when using the 'GroupId-Id' or 'GroupId-Name' parameter sets.
The ID of the dashboard to be copied. This parameter is mandatory when using the 'GroupId-Id' or 'GroupName-Id' parameter sets.
The name of the dashboard to be copied. This parameter is mandatory when using the 'GroupId-Name' or 'GroupName-Name' parameter sets.
A hashtable of tokens to be replaced in the dashboard. The key is the token name and the value is the new value. If not provided, the tokens from the source dashboard will be used.
An optional description for the new dashboard.
The name of the new dashboard.
The ID of the parent group for the new dashboard. This parameter is mandatory when using the 'GroupId-Id' or 'GroupId-Name' parameter sets.
The name of the parent group for the new dashboard. This parameter is mandatory when using the 'GroupName-Id' or 'GroupName-Name' parameter sets.

maml:alert.maml:para: Ensure that you are logged in before running any commands by using the Connect-LMAccount cmdlet.

maml:introduction.maml:para: ``` Copy-LMDashboard -Name "New Dashboard" -DashboardId 12345 -ParentGroupId 67890 Copies the dashboard with ID 12345 to a new dashboard named "New Dashboard" in the group with ID 67890. ```

``` Copy-LMDashboard -Name "New Dashboard" -DashboardName "Old Dashboard" -ParentGroupName "Group A" Copies the dashboard named "Old Dashboard" to a new dashboard named "New Dashboard" in the group named "Group A". ```

``` Copy-LMDashboard -Name "New Dashboard" -DashboardName "Old Dashboard" -ParentGroupName "Group A" -DashboardTokens @{ "defaultResourceName" = "Value1"; "defaultResourceGroup" = "Value2" } Copies the dashboard named "Old Dashboard" to a new dashboard named "New Dashboard" in the group named "Group A", replacing the tokens "defaultResourceName" with "Value1" and "defaultResourceGroup" with "Value2". ```

maml:description.maml:para: Creates a copy of a LogicMonitor device.

The Copy-LMDevice function creates a new device based on an existing device's configuration. It allows you to specify a new name, display name, and description while maintaining other settings from the source device.
An optional description for the new device.
The source device object to copy settings from. This parameter is mandatory.
The display name for the new device. If not specified, defaults to the Name parameter value.
The name for the new device. This parameter is mandatory.
An optional description for the new device.
The source device object to copy settings from. This parameter is mandatory.
The display name for the new device. If not specified, defaults to the Name parameter value.
The name for the new device. This parameter is mandatory.

dev:type.dev:name: None. You cannot pipe objects to this command.
Returns the newly created device object.

maml:alert.maml:para: Masked custom properties from the source device will need to be manually updated on the new device as they are not available via the API.

maml:introduction.maml:para: ``` #Copy a device with basic settings Copy-LMDevice -Name "NewDevice" -DeviceObject $deviceObject ```
``` #Copy a device with custom display name and description Copy-LMDevice -Name "NewDevice" -DisplayName "New Display Name" -Description "New device description" -DeviceObject $deviceObject ```

maml:description.maml:para: Copies device properties from a source device to target devices. Sensitive properties cannot be copied as their values are not available via API.

The Copy-LMDevicePropertyToDevice function copies specified properties from a source device to one or more target devices. The source device can be randomly selected from a group or explicitly specified. Properties are copied to the targets while preserving other existing device properties.
If specified, returns the updated device objects.
Array of property names to copy. These can be only be custom properties directly assigned to the device.
The ID of the source device to copy properties from. This parameter is part of the "SourceDevice" parameter set.
The ID of the target device(s) to copy properties to. Multiple device IDs can be specified.
If specified, returns the updated device objects.
Array of property names to copy. These can be only be custom properties directly assigned to the device.
The ID of the source group to randomly select a device from. This parameter is part of the "SourceGroup" parameter set.
The ID of the target device(s) to copy properties to. Multiple device IDs can be specified.
If specified, returns the updated device objects.
Array of property names to copy. These can be only be custom properties directly assigned to the device.
The ID of the source device to copy properties from. This parameter is part of the "SourceDevice" parameter set.
The ID of the source group to randomly select a device from. This parameter is part of the "SourceGroup" parameter set.
The ID of the target device(s) to copy properties to. Multiple device IDs can be specified.

maml:alert.maml:para: Requires an active Logic Monitor session. Use Connect-LMAccount to log in before running this function.

maml:introduction.maml:para: Copy-LMDevicePropertyToDevice -SourceDeviceId 123 -TargetDeviceId 456 -PropertyNames "location","department" Copies the location and department properties from device 123 to device 456.

Copy-LMDevicePropertyToDevice -SourceGroupId 789 -TargetDeviceId 456,457 -PropertyNames "location" -PassThru Randomly selects a device from group 789 and copies its location property to devices 456 and 457, returning the updated devices.

maml:description.maml:para: Copies device properties from a source device to target device groups. Sensitive properties cannot be copied as their values are not available via API.

The Copy-LMDevicePropertyToGroup function copies specified properties from a source device to one or more target device groups. The source device can be randomly selected from a group or explicitly specified. Properties are copied to the target groups while preserving other existing group properties.
If specified, returns the updated device group objects.
Array of property names to copy. These can be only be custom properties directly assigned to the device.
The ID of the source device to copy properties from. This parameter is part of the "SourceDevice" parameter set.
The ID of the target group(s) to copy properties to. Multiple group IDs can be specified.
If specified, returns the updated device group objects.
Array of property names to copy. These can be only be custom properties directly assigned to the device.
The ID of the source group to randomly select a device from. This parameter is part of the "SourceGroup" parameter set.
The ID of the target group(s) to copy properties to. Multiple group IDs can be specified.
If specified, returns the updated device group objects.
Array of property names to copy. These can be only be custom properties directly assigned to the device.
The ID of the source device to copy properties from. This parameter is part of the "SourceDevice" parameter set.
The ID of the source group to randomly select a device from. This parameter is part of the "SourceGroup" parameter set.
The ID of the target group(s) to copy properties to. Multiple group IDs can be specified.

maml:alert.maml:para: Requires an active Logic Monitor session. Use Connect-LMAccount to log in before running this function.

maml:introduction.maml:para: Copy-LMDevicePropertyToGroup -SourceDeviceId 123 -TargetGroupId 456 -PropertyNames "location","department" Copies the location and department properties from device 123 to group 456.

Copy-LMDevicePropertyToGroup -SourceGroupId 789 -TargetGroupId 456,457 -PropertyNames "location" -PassThru Randomly selects a device from group 789 and copies its location property to groups 456 and 457, returning the updated groups.

maml:description.maml:para: Creates a copy of a LogicMonitor report.

The Copy-LMReport function creates a new report based on an existing report's configuration. It allows you to specify a new name, description, and parent group while maintaining other settings from the source report.
An optional description for the new report.
The name for the new report. This parameter is mandatory.
The ID of the parent group for the new report.
The source report object to copy settings from. This parameter is mandatory.
An optional description for the new report.
The name for the new report. This parameter is mandatory.
The ID of the parent group for the new report.
The source report object to copy settings from. This parameter is mandatory.

Returns the newly created report object.

maml:alert.maml:para: You must run Connect-LMAccount before running this command.

maml:introduction.maml:para: ``` #Copy a report with basic settings Copy-LMReport -Name "New Report" -ReportObject $reportObject ```
``` #Copy a report with all optional parameters Copy-LMReport -Name "New Report" -Description "New report description" -ParentGroupId 12345 -ReportObject $reportObject ```

maml:description.maml:para: Disconnect from a previously connected Edwin account.
Disconnect-EAIAccount clears stored Edwin API credentials from the current PowerShell session.

maml:description.maml:para: Disconnects from a previously connected LM portal.
The Disconnect-LMAccount function clears stored API credentials for a previously connected LM portal. It's useful for switching between LM portals or clearing credentials after a script runs.

dev:type.dev:name: None. You cannot pipe objects to this command.
None. This command does not generate any output.

maml:alert.maml:para: Once disconnected you will need to reconnect to a portal before you will be allowed to run commands again.

maml:description.maml:para: Exports the latest version of device configurations from LogicMonitor.
The Export-LMDeviceConfigBackup function exports the latest version of device configurations for specified devices. It can export configs from either a single device or all devices in a device group.
A regex filter to use for filtering ConfigSource names. Defaults to ".*".
The ID of the device to export configurations from. This parameter is mandatory when using the Device parameter set.
A regex filter to use for filtering Instance names. Defaults to "running|current|PaloAlto".
The file path where the CSV backup will be exported to.
A regex filter to use for filtering ConfigSource names. Defaults to ".*".
The ID of the device group to export configurations from. This parameter is mandatory when using the DeviceGroup parameter set.
A regex filter to use for filtering Instance names. Defaults to "running|current|PaloAlto".
The file path where the CSV backup will be exported to.
A regex filter to use for filtering ConfigSource names. Defaults to ".*".
The ID of the device group to export configurations from. This parameter is mandatory when using the DeviceGroup parameter set.
The ID of the device to export configurations from. This parameter is mandatory when using the Device parameter set.
A regex filter to use for filtering Instance names. Defaults to "running|current|PaloAlto".
The file path where the CSV backup will be exported to.

dev:type.dev:name: None. You cannot pipe objects to this command.
Returns an array of device configuration objects if successful.

maml:alert.maml:para: You must run Connect-LMAccount before running this command.

maml:introduction.maml:para: ``` #Export configurations from a device group Export-LMDeviceConfigBackup -DeviceGroupId 2 -Path "export-report.csv" ```
``` #Export configurations from a single device Export-LMDeviceConfigBackup -DeviceId 1 -Path "export-report.csv" ```

maml:description.maml:para: Exports device monitoring data from LogicMonitor.

The Export-LMDeviceData function exports monitoring data from LogicMonitor devices or device groups. It supports exporting data for specific time ranges and can filter datasources. Data can be exported in CSV or JSON format.
A filter pattern to exclude specific datasources. Defaults to null (no exclusions).
A filter pattern to include specific datasources. Defaults to "*" (all datasources).
The ID of the device to export data from. This parameter is part of a mutually exclusive parameter set.
The end date and time for data collection. Defaults to current time.
The format for the exported data. Valid values are "csv", "json", or "none". Defaults to "none".
The path where exported files will be saved. Defaults to current directory.
The start date and time for data collection. Defaults to 1 hour ago.

A filter pattern to include specific datasources. Defaults to "*" (all datasources).

A filter pattern to include specific datasources. Defaults to "*" (all datasources).

maml:introduction.maml:para: ``` #Export device data to JSON Export-LMDeviceData -DeviceId 12345 -StartDate (Get-Date).AddDays(-1) -ExportFormat json ```

maml:description.maml:para: Exports LogicMonitor LogicModules for backup or transfer.
The Export-LMLogicModule function exports LogicModules from LogicMonitor. It supports exporting various types of modules including datasources, property rules, event sources, and more.
The path where the exported LogicModule will be saved. Defaults to current directory.
The ID of the LogicModule to export. This parameter is mandatory when using the Id parameter set.
The type of LogicModule to export. Valid values are: "datasources", "propertyrules", "eventsources", "topologysources", "configsources", "logsources", "functions", "oids".
The path where the exported LogicModule will be saved. Defaults to current directory.
The name of the LogicModule to export. This parameter is mandatory when using the Name parameter set.
The type of LogicModule to export. Valid values are: "datasources", "propertyrules", "eventsources", "topologysources", "configsources", "logsources", "functions", "oids".

maml:description.maml:para: Retrieves scheduled downtime entries from Edwin.

Get-EAISdt lists all scheduled downtime (SDT) entries for the connected Edwin organisation, or retrieves a single schedule when -Id is specified. A single schedule response includes the overrides array (epoch-second timestamps on read).
Schedule ID (UUID). When specified, retrieves a single schedule via GET /action/sdt/{id}.
Schedule ID (UUID). When specified, retrieves a single schedule via GET /action/sdt/{id}.

dev:type.dev:name: None. You cannot pipe objects to this command.
Returns Edwin.SDT objects.

maml:alert.maml:para: Use Connect-EAIAccount to establish an Edwin session before running this command. Requires ADMIN role or sdt_read API scope on the Edwin credentials.

maml:introduction.maml:para: Get-EAISdt
Get-EAISdt -Id '6d18821f-8f3c-48ca-8331-3e4c8d77cac3'
(Get-EAISdt -Id '6d18821f-8f3c-48ca-8331-3e4c8d77cac3').overrides

maml:description.maml:para: Retrieves calculated or realised Edwin SDT instances.
Get-EAISdtInstance lists downtime instances for a schedule within a time range, or retrieves a single instance by ID.
Exclusive range end. Defaults to seven days after StartTime when omitted.
Schedule ID (UUID). Required for the BySchedule parameter set unless piping an Edwin.SDT object.
Inclusive range start. Defaults to the current UTC time when omitted.
Instance ID in the format scheduleId:isoStartTime.
Exclusive range end. Defaults to seven days after StartTime when omitted.
Instance ID in the format scheduleId:isoStartTime.
Schedule ID (UUID). Required for the BySchedule parameter set unless piping an Edwin.SDT object.
Inclusive range start. Defaults to the current UTC time when omitted.

dev:type.dev:name: You can pipe Edwin.SDT objects (scheduleId)
System.String
Returns Edwin.SDT.Instance objects.

maml:alert.maml:para: Use Connect-EAIAccount before running this command. Use instance.originalInstanceId.startTime (not the top-level startTime) when creating overrides. The instanceId property is formatted as scheduleId:isoStartTime for use with Remove-EAISdtOverride.

maml:introduction.maml:para: Get-EAISdtInstance -ScheduleId '97038d1b-648a-4718-b287-33726ed49624'
Get-EAISdt -Id $id | Get-EAISdtInstance
$instances = Get-EAISdt -Id $id | Get-EAISdtInstance -EndTime (Get-Date).ToUniversalTime().AddDays(30) $instances | Format-Table instanceId, startTime, endTime, status

maml:description.maml:para: Retrieves audit logs from LogicMonitor.

The Get-LMAuditLog function retrieves audit logs from LogicMonitor based on the specified parameters. It supports retrieving logs by ID, by date range, or by applying filters. The function can retrieve up to 10000 logs in a single query.
The number of results to return per request. Must be between 1 and 1000. Defaults to 1000.
The end date for retrieving audit logs. Defaults to current time if not specified.
A string to filter audit logs by. Only logs containing this string will be returned.
The start date for retrieving audit logs. Defaults to 30 days ago if not specified.
The number of results to return per request. Must be between 1 and 1000. Defaults to 1000.
The ID of the specific audit log to retrieve. This parameter is part of a mutually exclusive parameter set.
The number of results to return per request. Must be between 1 and 1000. Defaults to 1000.
A filter object to apply when retrieving audit logs. Part of a mutually exclusive parameter set.
The number of results to return per request. Must be between 1 and 1000. Defaults to 1000.
The end date for retrieving audit logs. Defaults to current time if not specified.
A filter object to apply when retrieving audit logs. Part of a mutually exclusive parameter set.
The ID of the specific audit log to retrieve. This parameter is part of a mutually exclusive parameter set.
A string to filter audit logs by. Only logs containing this string will be returned.
The start date for retrieving audit logs. Defaults to 30 days ago if not specified.

dev:type.dev:name: None. You cannot pipe objects to this command.
Returns LogicMonitor.AuditLog objects.

maml:alert.maml:para: You must run Connect-LMAccount before running this command. Maximum of 10000 logs can be retrieved in a single query.

maml:introduction.maml:para: #Retrieve audit logs from the last week Get-LMAuditLog -StartDate (Get-Date).AddDays(-7)
#Search for specific audit logs Get-LMAuditLog -SearchString "login" -StartDate (Get-Date).AddDays(-30)

The Get-LMCollectorGroup function retrieves collector groups from LogicMonitor based on the specified parameters. It supports retrieving collector groups by ID, name, or using a filter. The function uses the LogicMonitor REST API to make the requests.

``` Get-LMCollectorGroup -Filter @{ Property = "Value" } Retrieves collector groups based on the specified filter criteria. ```

maml:description.maml:para: Downloads the LogicMonitor Collector installer.

The Get-LMCollectorInstaller function downloads the LogicMonitor Collector installer based on the specified parameters. It supports different operating systems, architectures, and collector sizes, and can download either standard or Early Access (EA) versions.
The path where the installer file will be saved. Defaults to the current directory.
The ID of the collector to download the installer for. This parameter is mandatory when using the Id parameter set.
The operating system and architecture for the installer. Valid values are 'Win64', 'Linux64'. Defaults to 'Win64'.
The size of the collector to install. Valid values are 'nano', 'small', 'medium', 'large', 'extra_large', 'double_extra_large'. Defaults to 'medium'.
Switch to use the Early Access version of the collector. Defaults to $false.
The path where the installer file will be saved. Defaults to the current directory.
The name of the collector to download the installer for. This parameter is mandatory when using the Name parameter set.
The operating system and architecture for the installer. Valid values are 'Win64', 'Linux64'. Defaults to 'Win64'.

maml:description.maml:para: Retrieves device information from LogicMonitor.

The Get-LMDevice function retrieves device information from LogicMonitor based on specified parameters. It can return a single device by ID or multiple devices based on name, display name, filter, or filter wizard. It also supports delta tracking for monitoring changes.
The number of results to return per request. Must be between 1 and 1000. Defaults to 1000.
Switch to return a deltaId along with the requested data for change tracking.
The number of results to return per request. Must be between 1 and 1000. Defaults to 1000.
The ID of the device to retrieve. Part of a mutually exclusive parameter set.
The number of results to return per request. Must be between 1 and 1000. Defaults to 1000.
Switch to return a deltaId along with the requested data for change tracking.
The display name of the device to retrieve. Part of a mutually exclusive parameter set.
The number of results to return per request. Must be between 1 and 1000. Defaults to 1000.
Switch to return a deltaId along with the requested data for change tracking.
The name of the device to retrieve. Part of a mutually exclusive parameter set.
The number of results to return per request. Must be between 1 and 1000. Defaults to 1000.
Switch to return a deltaId along with the requested data for change tracking.
A filter object to apply when retrieving devices. Part of a mutually exclusive parameter set.
The number of results to return per request. Must be between 1 and 1000. Defaults to 1000.
Switch to return a deltaId along with the requested data for change tracking.
Switch to use the filter wizard interface for building the filter. Part of a mutually exclusive parameter set.
The number of results to return per request. Must be between 1 and 1000. Defaults to 1000.
The deltaId string for retrieving changes since a previous query.
The number of results to return per request. Must be between 1 and 1000. Defaults to 1000.
Switch to return a deltaId along with the requested data for change tracking.
The deltaId string for retrieving changes since a previous query.
The display name of the device to retrieve. Part of a mutually exclusive parameter set.
A filter object to apply when retrieving devices. Part of a mutually exclusive parameter set.
Switch to use the filter wizard interface for building the filter. Part of a mutually exclusive parameter set.
The ID of the device to retrieve. Part of a mutually exclusive parameter set.
The name of the device to retrieve. Part of a mutually exclusive parameter set.

dev:type.dev:name: None. You cannot pipe objects to this command.
Returns LogicMonitor.Device objects.

maml:alert.maml:para: You must run Connect-LMAccount before running this command.

maml:introduction.maml:para: ``` #Retrieve a device by ID Get-LMDevice -Id 123 ```
``` #Retrieve devices with delta tracking Get-LMDevice -Delta ```

maml:description.maml:para: Retrieves alerts for a specific LogicMonitor device.

The Get-LMDeviceAlerts function retrieves all alerts associated with a specific device in LogicMonitor. The device can be identified by either ID or name, and the results can be filtered using custom criteria.
The number of results to return per request. Must be between 1 and 1000. Defaults to 1000.
A filter object to apply when retrieving alerts. This parameter is optional.
The ID of the device to retrieve alerts for. This parameter is mandatory when using the Id parameter set and can accept pipeline input.
The number of results to return per request. Must be between 1 and 1000. Defaults to 1000.
A filter object to apply when retrieving alerts. This parameter is optional.
The name of the device to retrieve alerts for. Part of a mutually exclusive parameter set.
The number of results to return per request. Must be between 1 and 1000. Defaults to 1000.
A filter object to apply when retrieving alerts. This parameter is optional.
The ID of the device to retrieve alerts for. This parameter is mandatory when using the Id parameter set and can accept pipeline input.
The name of the device to retrieve alerts for. Part of a mutually exclusive parameter set.

dev:type.dev:name: System.Int32. The device ID can be piped to this function.
System.Int32
Returns LogicMonitor.Alert objects.

maml:alert.maml:para: You must run Connect-LMAccount before running this command.

maml:introduction.maml:para: #Retrieve alerts for a device by ID Get-LMDeviceAlerts -Id 123
#Retrieve alerts for a device by name with filtering Get-LMDeviceAlerts -Name "Production-Server" -Filter $filterObject

maml:description.maml:para: Retrieves alert settings for a specific LogicMonitor device.

The Get-LMDeviceAlertSetting function retrieves the alert configuration settings for a specific device in LogicMonitor. The device can be identified by either ID or name, and the results can be filtered using custom criteria.

A filter object to apply when retrieving alert settings. This parameter is optional.
The ID of the device to retrieve alert settings for. This parameter is mandatory when using the Id parameter set and can accept pipeline input.
The number of results to return per request. Must be between 1 and 1000. Defaults to 1000.
A filter object to apply when retrieving alert settings. This parameter is optional.
The name of the device to retrieve alert settings for. Part of a mutually exclusive parameter set.

A filter object to apply when retrieving alert settings. This parameter is optional.
The ID of the device to retrieve alert settings for. This parameter is mandatory when using the Id parameter set and can accept pipeline input.
The name of the device to retrieve alert settings for. Part of a mutually exclusive parameter set.

dev:type.dev:name: System.Int32. The device ID can be piped to this function.
System.Int32
Returns LogicMonitor.AlertSetting objects.

maml:description.maml:para: Retrieves configuration source data for a LogicMonitor device.

The Get-LMDeviceConfigSourceData function retrieves configuration data from a specific device's configuration source in LogicMonitor. It supports retrieving full configurations or delta changes, and can return either all configs or just the latest one.
The ID of the host datasource. This parameter is mandatory.
The ID of the host datasource instance. This parameter is mandatory.
The ID of the device to retrieve configuration data for. This parameter is mandatory.
The specific configuration ID to retrieve. Part of a mutually exclusive parameter set.
The ID of the host datasource. This parameter is mandatory.
The ID of the host datasource instance. This parameter is mandatory.
The ID of the device to retrieve configuration data for. This parameter is mandatory.
The type of configuration to retrieve. Valid values are "Delta" or "Full". Defaults to "Delta".
The ID of the host datasource. This parameter is mandatory.
The ID of the host datasource instance. This parameter is mandatory.
The ID of the device to retrieve configuration data for. This parameter is mandatory.
Switch to retrieve only the most recent configuration. Part of the ListConfigs parameter set.
The specific configuration ID to retrieve. Part of a mutually exclusive parameter set.
The type of configuration to retrieve. Valid values are "Delta" or "Full". Defaults to "Delta".
The ID of the host datasource. This parameter is mandatory.
The ID of the host datasource instance. This parameter is mandatory.
The ID of the device to retrieve configuration data for. This parameter is mandatory.
Switch to retrieve only the most recent configuration. Part of the ListConfigs parameter set.

dev:type.dev:name: None. You cannot pipe objects to this command.
Returns configuration data objects.

maml:alert.maml:para: You must run Connect-LMAccount before running this command.

maml:introduction.maml:para: ``` #Retrieve latest configuration for a device Get-LMDeviceConfigSourceData -Id 123 -HdsId 456 -HdsInsId 789 -LatestConfigOnly ```
``` #Retrieve full configuration history Get-LMDeviceConfigSourceData -Id 123 -HdsId 456 -HdsInsId 789 -ConfigType Full ```

The start date and time for data collection. Defaults to 7 days ago if not specified.

maml:description.maml:para: Retrieves device group information from LogicMonitor.
The Get-LMDeviceGroup function retrieves device group information from a connected LogicMonitor portal. It supports retrieving groups by ID, name (including wildcards), or using custom filters.
The number of results to return per request. Must be between 1 and 1000. Defaults to 1000.
The number of results to return per request. Must be between 1 and 1000. Defaults to 1000.
The ID of the device group to retrieve. Part of a mutually exclusive parameter set.
The number of results to return per request. Must be between 1 and 1000. Defaults to 1000.
The name of the device group to retrieve. Supports wildcard input such as "* - Servers". Part of a mutually exclusive parameter set.
The number of results to return per request. Must be between 1 and 1000. Defaults to 1000.
A filter object to apply when retrieving device groups. Can include multiple conditions combined as AND operations. Part of a mutually exclusive parameter set.
The number of results to return per request. Must be between 1 and 1000. Defaults to 1000.
Switch to use the filter wizard interface for building the filter. Part of a mutually exclusive parameter set.
The number of results to return per request. Must be between 1 and 1000. Defaults to 1000.
A filter object to apply when retrieving device groups. Can include multiple conditions combined as AND operations. Part of a mutually exclusive parameter set.
Switch to use the filter wizard interface for building the filter. Part of a mutually exclusive parameter set.
The ID of the device group to retrieve. Part of a mutually exclusive parameter set.
The name of the device group to retrieve. Supports wildcard input such as "* - Servers". Part of a mutually exclusive parameter set.

maml:alert.maml:para: You must run Connect-LMAccount before running this command. When using filters, consult the LM API docs for allowed filter fields.

maml:introduction.maml:para: ``` #Retrieve all device groups Get-LMDeviceGroup ```
``` #Retrieve a specific device group by name with wildcard Get-LMDeviceGroup -Name "* - Servers" ```
``` #Retrieve device groups using a filter Get-LMDeviceGroup -Filter @{parentId=1;disableAlerting=$false} ```

maml:description.maml:para: Retrieves data for LogicMonitor device instances.

The Get-LMDeviceInstanceData function retrieves monitoring data for specified device instances in LogicMonitor. It supports data aggregation and time range filtering, with a maximum timeframe of 24 hours.
The type of aggregation to apply to the data. Valid values are "first", "last", "min", "max", "sum", "average", "none". Defaults to "none".
The end date for the data retrieval. Defaults to current time if not specified.
An array of device instance IDs for which to retrieve data. This parameter is mandatory and can be specified using the Id alias.
The period for data aggregation. Defaults to 1 as this appears to be the only supported value.
The start date for the data retrieval. Defaults to 24 hours ago if not specified.
The type of aggregation to apply to the data. Valid values are "first", "last", "min", "max", "sum", "average", "none". Defaults to "none".
The end date for the data retrieval. Defaults to current time if not specified.
An array of device instance IDs for which to retrieve data. This parameter is mandatory and can be specified using the Id alias.
The period for data aggregation. Defaults to 1 as this appears to be the only supported value.
The start date for the data retrieval. Defaults to 24 hours ago if not specified.

dev:type.dev:name: None. You cannot pipe objects to this command.
Returns an array of data points for the specified device instances.

maml:alert.maml:para: You must run Connect-LMAccount before running this command. Maximum time range for data retrieval is 24 hours.

maml:introduction.maml:para: #Retrieve data for multiple instances with aggregation Get-LMDeviceInstanceData -Ids "12345","67890" -AggregationType "average" -StartDate (Get-Date).AddHours(-12)
#Retrieve raw data for a single instance Get-LMDeviceInstanceData -Id "12345" -AggregationType "none"

The Get-LMLogMessage function retrieves log messages from LogicMonitor based on specified time ranges or date ranges. It supports both synchronous and asynchronous queries with pagination control.
Switch to enable asynchronous query mode.
The number of results to return per request. Must be between 1 and 300. Defaults to 300.

maml:description.maml:para: Retrieves the LogicMonitor portal version from API response headers.

The Get-LMPortalVersion function makes a lightweight API call to retrieve the portal version from the x-server-version response header. The version is cached in $Script:LMAuth.Version for the duration of the session to avoid repeated API calls.
Forces a fresh API call to retrieve the version, bypassing the cached value.
Forces a fresh API call to retrieve the version, bypassing the cached value.

dev:type.dev:name: Returns a PSCustomObject with RawVersion (string), Major (int), and Minor (int) properties.

maml:alert.maml:para: This is a private function used internally by the module.

maml:introduction.maml:para: ``` $version = Get-LMPortalVersion Write-Host "Portal version: $($version.RawVersion)" ```

maml:description.maml:para: Retrieves recently deleted resources from the LogicMonitor recycle bin.

The Get-LMRecentlyDeleted function queries the LogicMonitor recycle bin for deleted resources within a configurable time range. Results can be filtered by resource type and deleted-by user, and support paging through the API using size, offset, and sort parameters.
The number of records to request per API call (1-1000). Defaults to 1000.
The earliest deletion timestamp (inclusive) to return. Defaults to seven days prior when not specified.
The latest deletion timestamp (exclusive) to return. Defaults to the current time when not specified.
Limits results to items deleted by the specified user principal.
Limits results to a specific resource type. Accepted values are All, device, and deviceGroup. Defaults to All.
Sort expression passed to the API. Defaults to -deletedOn.
The number of records to request per API call (1-1000). Defaults to 1000.
The earliest deletion timestamp (inclusive) to return. Defaults to seven days prior when not specified.
The latest deletion timestamp (exclusive) to return. Defaults to the current time when not specified.
Limits results to items deleted by the specified user principal.
Limits results to a specific resource type. Accepted values are All, device, and deviceGroup. Defaults to All.
Sort expression passed to the API. Defaults to -deletedOn.

maml:alert.maml:para: You must establish a session with Connect-LMAccount prior to calling this function.

maml:introduction.maml:para: Get-LMRecentlyDeleted -ResourceType device -DeletedBy "lmsupport"
Retrieves every device deleted by the user lmsupport over the past seven days.
Get-LMRecentlyDeleted -DeletedAfter (Get-Date).AddDays(-1) -DeletedBefore (Get-Date) -BatchSize 100 -Sort "+deletedOn"
Retrieves deleted resources from the past 24 hours in ascending order of deletion time.

maml:description.maml:para: Retrieves LogicMonitor Uptime devices from the v3 device endpoint.

The Get-LMUptimeDevice cmdlet returns Uptime monitors (web or ping) that are backed by the LogicMonitor v3 /device/devices endpoint. It supports lookup by ID or name, optional filtering by internal/external flag, custom filters, and the interactive filter wizard. The Type parameter is optional and filters by webcheck or pingcheck uptime devices. If not specified, both types are returned.

LogicMonitor.LMUptimeDevice

maml:alert.maml:para: You must run Connect-LMAccount before invoking this cmdlet. Responses are tagged with the LogicMonitor.LMUptimeDevice type information.

maml:introduction.maml:para: ``` Get-LMUptimeDevice ```
Retrieves all Uptime devices (both webcheck and pingcheck) across the account.
``` Get-LMUptimeDevice -Type uptimewebcheck ```
Retrieves all web Uptime devices across the account.
``` Get-LMUptimeDevice -Type uptimewebcheck -IsInternal $true ```
Retrieves internal web Uptime devices only.
``` Get-LMUptimeDevice -Name "web-int-01" ```
Retrieves a specific Uptime device by name.
``` Get-LMUptimeDevice -Id 123 ```
Retrieves a specific Uptime device by ID.

maml:description.maml:para: Imports devices from a CSV file into LogicMonitor.

The Import-LMDevicesFromCSV function imports devices from a CSV file into LogicMonitor. It requires a valid CSV file containing device information such as IP address, display name, host group, collector ID, and description. The function checks if the user is logged in and has valid API credentials before importing the devices.
Specifies the auto-balanced collector group ID to assign to the imported devices. This parameter is optional and can be used with the 'Import' parameter set.
Specifies the collector ID to assign to the imported devices. This parameter is optional and can be used with the 'Import' parameter set.
Specifies the path to the CSV file containing the device information. This parameter is mandatory when the 'Import' parameter set is used.
Indicates whether to return the imported devices as output. This parameter is optional and can be used with the 'Import' parameter set.
Generates an example CSV file with sample device information. This parameter is optional and can be used with the 'Sample' parameter set.
Specifies the auto-balanced collector group ID to assign to the imported devices. This parameter is optional and can be used with the 'Import' parameter set.
Specifies the collector ID to assign to the imported devices. This parameter is optional and can be used with the 'Import' parameter set.
Specifies the path to the CSV file containing the device information. This parameter is mandatory when the 'Import' parameter set is used.
Generates an example CSV file with sample device information. This parameter is optional and can be used with the 'Sample' parameter set.
Indicates whether to return the imported devices as output. This parameter is optional and can be used with the 'Import' parameter set.

maml:alert.maml:para: - This function requires valid API credentials to connect to LogicMonitor. - The CSV file must have the following columns: ip, displayname, hostgroup. collectorid, collectorgroupid, description, property.name1, property.name2.. are optional. - The function creates device groups if they don't exist based on the host group path specified in the CSV file. - If the collector ID is not specified in the CSV file, the function uses the collector ID specified by the CollectorId parameter.

maml:introduction.maml:para: Import-LMDevicesFromCSV -FilePath "C:\Devices.csv" -CollectorId 1234 Imports devices from the "Devices.csv" file located at "C:\Devices.csv" and assigns the collector with ID 1234 to the imported devices.
Import-LMDevicesFromCSV -GenerateExampleCSV Generates an example CSV file named "SampleLMDeviceImportCSV.csv" in the current directory with sample device information.

The Import-LMLogicModule function imports a LogicModule from a file path or file data. Supports various module types including datasource, propertyrules, eventsource, topologysource, configsource, logsource, functions, and oids.
The path to the file containing the LogicModule to import.

The type of LogicModule. Valid values are "datasource", "propertyrules", "eventsource", "topologysource", "configsource", "logsource", "functions", "oids". Defaults to "datasource".
The file data of the LogicModule to import.

The type of LogicModule. Valid values are "datasource", "propertyrules", "eventsource", "topologysource", "configsource", "logsource", "functions", "oids". Defaults to "datasource".

maml:introduction.maml:para: #Import a datasource module Import-LMLogicModule -FilePath "C:\LogicModules\datasource.xml" -Type "datasource" -ForceOverwrite $true
#Import a property rules module Import-LMLogicModule -File $fileData -Type "propertyrules"

maml:description.maml:para: Imports a LogicModule into LogicMonitor using the V2 import endpoints.

The Import-LMLogicModuleFromFile function imports a LogicModule from a file path or file data using the new XML and JSON import endpoints. Supports various module types including datasources, configsources, eventsources, batchjobs, logsources, oids, topologysources, functions, and diagnosticsources.

Optional. Comma-separated list of fields to preserve during import. Only applies to JSON imports. Defaults to preserving none of the fields. Valid values are "NAME", "APPLIES_TO_SCRIPT", "COLLECTION_INTERVAL", "ACTIVE_DISCOVERY_INTERVAL", "ACTIVE_DISCOVERY_FILTERS", "MODULE_GROUP", "DISPLAY_NAME", "USE_WILD_VALUE_AS_UUID", "DATAPOINT_ALERT_THRESHOLDS", "TAGS". "NAME" will preserve the name of the LogicModule. "APPLIES_TO_SCRIPT" will preserve the appliesToScript of the LogicModule. "COLLECTION_INTERVAL" will preserve the collectionInterval of the LogicModule. "ACTIVE_DISCOVERY_INTERVAL" will preserve the activeDiscoveryInterval of the LogicModule. "ACTIVE_DISCOVERY_FILTERS" will preserve the activeDiscoveryFilters of the LogicModule. "MODULE_GROUP" will preserve the moduleGroup of the LogicModule. "DISPLAY_NAME" will preserve the displayName of the LogicModule. "USE_WILD_VALUE_AS_UUID" will preserve the useWildValueAsUuid of the LogicModule. "DATAPOINT_ALERT_THRESHOLDS" will preserve the datapointAlertThresholds of the LogicModule. "TAGS" will preserve the tags of the LogicModule.
The path to the file containing the LogicModule to import. The function will read the file content automatically.
The format of the LogicModule file. Valid values are "xml" or "json". Defaults to "json".

Optional. Specifies how to handle conflicts during import. Only applies to JSON imports. Defaults to "FORCE_OVERWRITE". Valid values are "FORCE_OVERWRITE" or "ERROR". "FORCE_OVERWRITE" will overwrite the existing LogicModule with the same name. "ERROR" will throw an error if a conflict is found.

The type of LogicModule. Valid values are "datasources", "configsources", "eventsources", "batchjobs", "logsources", "oids", "topologysources", "functions", "diagnosticsources". Defaults to "datasources".

Optional. Comma-separated list of fields to preserve during import. Only applies to JSON imports. Defaults to preserving none of the fields. Valid values are "NAME", "APPLIES_TO_SCRIPT", "COLLECTION_INTERVAL", "ACTIVE_DISCOVERY_INTERVAL", "ACTIVE_DISCOVERY_FILTERS", "MODULE_GROUP", "DISPLAY_NAME", "USE_WILD_VALUE_AS_UUID", "DATAPOINT_ALERT_THRESHOLDS", "TAGS". "NAME" will preserve the name of the LogicModule. "APPLIES_TO_SCRIPT" will preserve the appliesToScript of the LogicModule. "COLLECTION_INTERVAL" will preserve the collectionInterval of the LogicModule. "ACTIVE_DISCOVERY_INTERVAL" will preserve the activeDiscoveryInterval of the LogicModule. "ACTIVE_DISCOVERY_FILTERS" will preserve the activeDiscoveryFilters of the LogicModule. "MODULE_GROUP" will preserve the moduleGroup of the LogicModule. "DISPLAY_NAME" will preserve the displayName of the LogicModule. "USE_WILD_VALUE_AS_UUID" will preserve the useWildValueAsUuid of the LogicModule. "DATAPOINT_ALERT_THRESHOLDS" will preserve the datapointAlertThresholds of the LogicModule. "TAGS" will preserve the tags of the LogicModule.
The raw file content of the LogicModule to import as a string. Use Get-Content with -Raw parameter to read file content properly (e.g., Get-Content 'file.json' -Raw).
The format of the LogicModule file. Valid values are "xml" or "json". Defaults to "json".

Optional. Specifies how to handle conflicts during import. Only applies to JSON imports. Defaults to "FORCE_OVERWRITE". Valid values are "FORCE_OVERWRITE" or "ERROR". "FORCE_OVERWRITE" will overwrite the existing LogicModule with the same name. "ERROR" will throw an error if a conflict is found.

The type of LogicModule. Valid values are "datasources", "configsources", "eventsources", "batchjobs", "logsources", "oids", "topologysources", "functions", "diagnosticsources". Defaults to "datasources".

Optional. Comma-separated list of fields to preserve during import. Only applies to JSON imports. Defaults to preserving none of the fields. Valid values are "NAME", "APPLIES_TO_SCRIPT", "COLLECTION_INTERVAL", "ACTIVE_DISCOVERY_INTERVAL", "ACTIVE_DISCOVERY_FILTERS", "MODULE_GROUP", "DISPLAY_NAME", "USE_WILD_VALUE_AS_UUID", "DATAPOINT_ALERT_THRESHOLDS", "TAGS". "NAME" will preserve the name of the LogicModule. "APPLIES_TO_SCRIPT" will preserve the appliesToScript of the LogicModule. "COLLECTION_INTERVAL" will preserve the collectionInterval of the LogicModule. "ACTIVE_DISCOVERY_INTERVAL" will preserve the activeDiscoveryInterval of the LogicModule. "ACTIVE_DISCOVERY_FILTERS" will preserve the activeDiscoveryFilters of the LogicModule. "MODULE_GROUP" will preserve the moduleGroup of the LogicModule. "DISPLAY_NAME" will preserve the displayName of the LogicModule. "USE_WILD_VALUE_AS_UUID" will preserve the useWildValueAsUuid of the LogicModule. "DATAPOINT_ALERT_THRESHOLDS" will preserve the datapointAlertThresholds of the LogicModule. "TAGS" will preserve the tags of the LogicModule.
The raw file content of the LogicModule to import as a string. Use Get-Content with -Raw parameter to read file content properly (e.g., Get-Content 'file.json' -Raw).
The path to the file containing the LogicModule to import. The function will read the file content automatically.
The format of the LogicModule file. Valid values are "xml" or "json". Defaults to "json".

Optional. Specifies how to handle conflicts during import. Only applies to JSON imports. Defaults to "FORCE_OVERWRITE". Valid values are "FORCE_OVERWRITE" or "ERROR". "FORCE_OVERWRITE" will overwrite the existing LogicModule with the same name. "ERROR" will throw an error if a conflict is found.

The type of LogicModule. Valid values are "datasources", "configsources", "eventsources", "batchjobs", "logsources", "oids", "topologysources", "functions", "diagnosticsources". Defaults to "datasources".

dev:type.dev:name: None. You cannot pipe objects to this command.
Returns a success message if the import is successful.
System.String

maml:alert.maml:para:
You must run Connect-LMAccount before running this command. Requires PowerShell version 6.1 or higher.
Note: Some module types only support specific formats: - logsources, oids, functions, diagnosticsources: JSON only - Other types: Both XML and JSON supported

maml:introduction.maml:para: #Import a datasource module from XML Import-LMLogicModuleFromFile -FilePath "C:\LogicModules\datasource.xml" -Type "datasources" -Format "xml"
#Import a logsource module from JSON with conflict handling Import-LMLogicModuleFromFile -FilePath "C:\LogicModules\logsource.json" -Type "logsources" -Format "json" -HandleConflict "FORCE_OVERWRITE"

#Import an eventsource from file data (read file content first with -Raw parameter) $fileData = Get-Content -Path "C:\LogicModules\eventsource.xml" -Raw Import-LMLogicModuleFromFile -File $fileData -Type "eventsources" -Format "xml"
#Import with fields to preserve Import-LMLogicModuleFromFile -FilePath "C:\LogicModules\datasource.json" -Type "datasources" -Format "json" -FieldsToPreserve "description,appliesTo"

maml:description.maml:para: Executes a custom LogicMonitor API request with full control over endpoint and payload.

The Invoke-LMAPIRequest function provides advanced users with direct access to the LogicMonitor API while leveraging the module's authentication, retry logic, debug utilities, and error handling. This is useful for accessing API endpoints that don't yet have dedicated cmdlets in the module.
Switch to return the response as a hashtable instead of a PSCustomObject.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
The Content-Type header for the request. Defaults to "application/json".
Optional hashtable containing the request body data. Will be automatically converted to JSON. Use this for POST, PATCH, and PUT requests.
Maximum number of retry attempts for transient errors. Defaults to 3. Set to 0 to disable retries.
The HTTP method to use. Valid values: GET, POST, PATCH, PUT, DELETE.
Switch to completely disable retry logic and fail immediately on any error.
Path to save the response content to a file. Useful for downloading reports or exports.
Optional hashtable of query parameters to append to the request URL. Example: @{ size = 100; offset = 0; filter = 'name:"test"' }
The API resource path (e.g., "/device/devices", "/setting/integrations/123"). Do not include the base URL or query parameters here.
Optional type name to add to the returned objects (e.g., "LogicMonitor.CustomResource"). This enables proper formatting if you have custom format definitions.
The X-Version header value for the API request. Defaults to 3. Some newer API endpoints may require different version numbers.
Runs the command in a mode that only reports what would happen without performing the actions.
Switch to return the response as a hashtable instead of a PSCustomObject.

Optional raw string body to send with the request. Use this instead of -Data when you need complete control over the request body format. Mutually exclusive with -Data.
The API resource path (e.g., "/device/devices", "/setting/integrations/123"). Do not include the base URL or query parameters here.

System.Collections.Hashtable

maml:alert.maml:para:
You must run Connect-LMAccount before running this command.
This cmdlet is designed for advanced users who need to: - Access API endpoints not yet covered by dedicated cmdlets - Test new API features or beta endpoints - Implement custom workflows requiring direct API access - Prototype new functionality before requesting cmdlet additions
For standard operations, use the dedicated cmdlets (Get-LMDevice, New-LMDevice, etc.) as they provide better parameter validation, documentation, and user experience.

maml:introduction.maml:para: # Get a custom resource not yet supported by a dedicated cmdlet Invoke-LMAPIRequest -ResourcePath "/setting/integrations" -Method GET

# Create a resource with custom payload $data = @{ name = "My Integration" type = "slack" url = "https://hooks.slack.com/services/..." } Invoke-LMAPIRequest -ResourcePath "/setting/integrations" -Method POST -Data $data
# Delete a resource Invoke-LMAPIRequest -ResourcePath "/setting/integrations/456" -Method DELETE

# Get with query parameters and custom version $queryParams = @{ size = 500 filter = 'status:"active"' fields = "id,name,status" } Invoke-LMAPIRequest -ResourcePath "/device/devices" -Method GET -QueryParams $queryParams -Version 3
# Use raw body for special formatting requirements $rawJson = '{"name":"test","customField":null}' Invoke-LMAPIRequest -ResourcePath "/custom/endpoint" -Method POST -RawBody $rawJson
# Download a report to file Invoke-LMAPIRequest -ResourcePath "/report/reports/123/download" -Method GET -OutFile "C:\Reports\report.pdf"
# Format output as table Invoke-LMAPIRequest -ResourcePath "/device/devices" -Method GET | Format-Table id, name, displayName, status

maml:description.maml:para: Triggers the execution of a LogicMonitor report.

Invoke-LMReportExecution starts an on-demand run of a LogicMonitor report. The report can be identified by ID or name. Optional parameters allow impersonating another admin or overriding the email recipients for the generated output.
The ID of the report to execute.
One or more email addresses (comma-separated) that should receive the generated report.
The admin ID to impersonate when generating the report. Defaults to the current user when omitted.
The name of the report to execute.

maml:alert.maml:para: You must run Connect-LMAccount before running this command.

maml:introduction.maml:para: ``` Invoke-LMReportExecution -Id 42 ```
Starts an immediate execution of the report with ID 42 using the current user's context.
``` Invoke-LMReportExecution -Name "Monthly Availability" -WithAdminId 101 -ReceiveEmails "ops@example.com" ```
Runs the "Monthly Availability" report as admin ID 101 and emails the results to ops@example.com.

End date and time. Required for one-time range schedules.
End hour (0-23) for recurring SDTs.
End minute (0-59) for recurring SDTs.

maml:description.maml:para: Adds an override to a single Edwin SDT instance.
New-EAISdtOverride skips or reschedules one occurrence of a schedule without changing the underlying schedule definition.
Use -Skip to skip an instance. Use -NewStartTime and -NewEndTime to reschedule it.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
New end time for an adjusted instance.
New start time for an adjusted instance.
Canonical original instance start time. Used with -ScheduleId when not piping an instance.
Re-fetches and returns the parent schedule after the override is added.
Schedule ID (UUID). Optional when piping an Edwin.SDT.Instance object.
Optional note stored with the override.
Runs the command in a mode that only reports what would happen without performing the actions.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Canonical original instance start time. Used with -ScheduleId when not piping an instance.

Runs the command in a mode that only reports what would happen without performing the actions.

dev:type.dev:name: You can pipe Edwin.SDT.Instance objects from Get-EAISdtInstance.
System.Object
None by default. With -PassThru

maml:alert.maml:para:
Always pipe or filter to the specific instance you intend to change. Derive -NewStartTime and -NewEndTime from the piped instance, not from Get-Date.
Use instance.originalInstanceId.startTime as the canonical original start time. The instanceId property is formatted as scheduleId:isoStartTime for use with Remove-EAISdtOverride.

maml:introduction.maml:para: $instances = Get-EAISdt -Id $id | Get-EAISdtInstance $instances | Where-Object { $_.instanceId -like '*2026-07-22T16:00:00.000Z*' } | New-EAISdtOverride -Skip -Summary 'Holiday'

$instance = Get-EAISdt -Id $id | Get-EAISdtInstance | Where-Object { $_.instanceId -like '*2026-07-22T16:00:00.000Z*' } $instance | New-EAISdtOverride ` -NewStartTime $instance.originalInstanceId.startTime ` -NewEndTime $instance.originalInstanceId.startTime.AddHours(4)

Start minute (0-59) for recurring SDTs.

The New-LMLogicModule function creates a new Logic Module in LogicMonitor. It supports various types of modules including datasources, property rules, topology sources, event sources, log sources, and config sources.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
A PSCustomObject containing the Logic Module configuration. Must follow the schema model defined in LogicMonitor's API documentation.
The type of Logic Module to create. Valid values are: datasources, propertyrules, topologysources, eventsources, logsources, configsources
Runs the command in a mode that only reports what would happen without performing the actions.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
A PSCustomObject containing the Logic Module configuration. Must follow the schema model defined in LogicMonitor's API documentation.
The type of Logic Module to create. Valid values are: datasources, propertyrules, topologysources, eventsources, logsources, configsources
Runs the command in a mode that only reports what would happen without performing the actions.

dev:type.dev:name: None. You cannot pipe objects to this command.
Returns LogicMonitor object of the appropriate type based on the Type parameter: LogicMonitor.Datasource

maml:alert.maml:para: You must run Connect-LMAccount before running this command. For Logic Module schema details, see: https://www.logicmonitor.com/swagger-ui-master/api-v3/dist/#/Datasources/addDatasourceById

maml:description.maml:para: Creates a new LogicMonitor Log Partition.
The New-LMLogPartition function creates a new LogicMonitor Log Partition.
When true, ingestion automatically restarts on contract renewal.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
The contract interval in hours. 0 = monthly (1st of next month), 24 = daily, 168 = weekly (next Monday), or a custom hour value.
The description of the new log partition.
The name of the new log partition.
The retention in days of the new log partition.
The sku of the new log partition. If not specified, the sku from the default log partition will be used. Its recommended to not specify this parameter and let the function determine the sku.
The status of the new log partition. Possible values are "active" or "inactive".
The tenant of the new log partition.
Runs the command in a mode that only reports what would happen without performing the actions.
When true, ingestion automatically restarts on contract renewal.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.

The description of the new log partition.
The name of the new log partition.
The retention in days of the new log partition.
The sku of the new log partition. If not specified, the sku from the default log partition will be used. Its recommended to not specify this parameter and let the function determine the sku.
The status of the new log partition. Possible values are "active" or "inactive".
When true, ingestion stops when the usage limit is exceeded. Use with the UsageLimit parameter set together with UsageLimit.
The tenant of the new log partition.
The usage limit for the log partition contract, for example 100GB. Use with the UsageLimit parameter set together with StopIngestionOnLimit.
Runs the command in a mode that only reports what would happen without performing the actions.
When true, ingestion automatically restarts on contract renewal.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
The contract interval in hours. 0 = monthly (1st of next month), 24 = daily, 168 = weekly (next Monday), or a custom hour value.
The description of the new log partition.
The name of the new log partition.
The retention in days of the new log partition.
The sku of the new log partition. If not specified, the sku from the default log partition will be used. Its recommended to not specify this parameter and let the function determine the sku.
The status of the new log partition. Possible values are "active" or "inactive".
When true, ingestion stops when the usage limit is exceeded. Use with the UsageLimit parameter set together with UsageLimit.
The tenant of the new log partition.
The usage limit for the log partition contract, for example 100GB. Use with the UsageLimit parameter set together with StopIngestionOnLimit.
Runs the command in a mode that only reports what would happen without performing the actions.

dev:type.dev:name: None. You cannot pipe objects to this command.
Returns LogicMonitor.LogPartition object.

maml:alert.maml:para: You must run Connect-LMAccount before running this command.

maml:introduction.maml:para: #Create a new log partition New-LMLogPartition -Name "customerA" -Description "Customer A Log Partition" -Retention 30 -Status "active" -Tenant "customerA"

#Create a new log partition with usage limit controls New-LMLogPartition -Name "customerA" -Description "Customer A Log Partition" -Retention 30 -Status "active" -Tenant "customerA" -UsageLimit "100GB" -StopIngestionOnLimit $true -AutoRestartOnRenewal $true

maml:description.maml:para: Creates a new network scan in LogicMonitor.
The New-LMNetScan function is used to create a new network scan in LogicMonitor. It sends a POST request to the LogicMonitor API to create the network scan with the specified parameters.
The token to use for changing the name of discovered devices. The default value is "##REVERSEDNS##".
The ID of the collector to use for the network scan. This parameter is mandatory.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
The ID of the credential group to use for the network scan.
The name of the credential group to use for the network scan.
The description of the network scan.
The type of duplicate exclusion to apply. The default value is "1".
Specifies whether to ignore duplicate system IPs. The default value is $false.
The method to use for the network scan. Only "nmap" is supported. The default value is "nmap".
The name of the network scan. This parameter is mandatory.
The ID of the network scan group to assign the network scan to. The default value is "1".
The next start time for the network scan. The default value is "manual".
The next start time epoch for the network scan. The default value is "0".
PSCustomObject containing schedule configuration for the netscan. If not provided, defaults to manual execution.
The subnet range to scan. This parameter is mandatory.
Runs the command in a mode that only reports what would happen without performing the actions.

The ID of the collector to use for the network scan. This parameter is mandatory.

The name of the network scan. This parameter is mandatory.
The ID of the network scan group to assign the network scan to. The default value is "1".
The next start time for the network scan. The default value is "manual".
The next start time epoch for the network scan. The default value is "0".
PSCustomObject containing schedule configuration for the netscan. If not provided, defaults to manual execution.
The subnet range to scan. This parameter is mandatory.
Runs the command in a mode that only reports what would happen without performing the actions.

maml:introduction.maml:para: New-LMNetScan -CollectorId "12345" -Name "MyNetScan" -SubnetRange "192.168.0.0/24" Creates a new network scan with the specified collector ID, name, and subnet range.

maml:description.maml:para: Creates normalized properties in LogicMonitor.

maml:description.maml:para: Creates a new LogicMonitor OpsNote.
The New-LMOpsNote function is used to create a new OpsNote in LogicMonitor. OpsNotes are used to document important information or events related to monitoring.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Specifies an array of device group IDs to associate with the OpsNote.
Specifies an array of device IDs to associate with the OpsNote.
Specifies the content of the OpsNote. This parameter is mandatory.
Specifies the date and time of the OpsNote. If not provided, the current date and time will be used.
Specifies an array of tags to associate with the OpsNote.
Specifies an array of website IDs to associate with the OpsNote.
Runs the command in a mode that only reports what would happen without performing the actions.

maml:introduction.maml:para: New-LMOpsNote -Note "Server maintenance scheduled for tomorrow" -NoteDate (Get-Date).AddDays(1) -Tags "maintenance", "server"

This example creates a new OpsNote with the content "Server maintenance scheduled for tomorrow" and a note date set to tomorrow. It also associates the tags "maintenance" and "server" with the OpsNote.

maml:description.maml:para: Creates a new data point object for pushing metric data to LogicMonitor.

The New-LMPushMetricDataPoint function creates a new data point object that can be used to push metric data to LogicMonitor. The function accepts an array of data points, where each data point consists of a name and a value. The function also allows you to specify the data point type, aggregation type, and percentile value.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Specifies the aggregation type of the data point. Valid values are "min", "max", "avg", "sum", "none", and "percentile". The default value is "none".
A mandatory parameter that accepts an array of data points. Each data point should be an object with a Name and a Value property.
An optional parameter that allows you to pass an existing array of data points. If not provided, a new array will be created.
Specifies the type of the data point. Valid values are "counter", "derive", and "gauge". The default value is "gauge".
Specifies the percentile value for the data point. This parameter is only applicable when the DataPointAggregationType is set to "percentile". The value should be between 0 and 100.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Specifies the aggregation type of the data point. Valid values are "min", "max", "avg", "sum", "none", and "percentile". The default value is "none".
A mandatory parameter that accepts an array of data points. Each data point should be an object with a Name and a Value property.
An optional parameter that allows you to pass an existing array of data points. If not provided, a new array will be created.
Specifies the type of the data point. Valid values are "counter", "derive", and "gauge". The default value is "gauge".
Specifies the percentile value for the data point. This parameter is only applicable when the DataPointAggregationType is set to "percentile". The value should be between 0 and 100.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.

dev:type.dev:name: None. You cannot pipe objects to this command.
Returns LogicMonitor.DataPoint object.

maml:alert.maml:para: You must run Connect-LMAccount before running this command.

maml:introduction.maml:para: New-LMPushMetricDataPoint -DataPoints $datapoints -DataPointType "gauge" -DataPointAggregationType "avg"
This example creates two data points for CPU usage and memory usage, and sets the data point type to "gauge" and the aggregation type to "avg".

maml:description.maml:para: Creates a new instance of a LogicMonitor push metric.
The New-LMPushMetricInstance function is used to create a new instance of a LogicMonitor push metric. It adds the instance to the specified instances array and returns the updated array.

The list of datapoints associated with the new instance. Datapoints should be the results of the New-LMPushMetricDataPoint function.
The description of the new instance.
The display name of the new instance. If not specified, the InstanceName will be used as the display name.
The name of the new instance.
A hashtable containing additional properties for the new instance.
The array of existing instances to which the new instance will be added.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.

maml:introduction.maml:para: ``` $instances = New-LMPushMetricInstance -InstancesArrary $instances -InstanceName "Instance1" -InstanceDisplayName "Instance 1" -InstanceDescription "This is instance 1" -InstanceProperties @{Property1 = "Value1"; Property2 = "Value2"} -Datapoints $datapoints ```
This example creates a new instance with the specified parameters and adds it to the existing instances array.

maml:description.maml:para: Creates a new LogicMonitor recipient object.
The New-LMRecipient function creates a new LogicMonitor recipient object that can be used with recipient groups. The recipient can be an admin user, arbitrary email, or another recipient group.
The address of the recipient. For ADMIN/ARBITRARY this is an email address, for GROUP this is the group name.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Optional contact information for the recipient.
The notification method for ADMIN recipients. Not used for GROUP type. Possible values: email, sms, voice, smsemail or the name of an existing integration
The type of recipient. Must be one of: ADMIN, ARBITRARY, or GROUP.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
The address of the recipient. For ADMIN/ARBITRARY this is an email address, for GROUP this is the group name.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Optional contact information for the recipient.
The notification method for ADMIN recipients. Not used for GROUP type. Possible values: email, sms, voice, smsemail or the name of an existing integration
The type of recipient. Must be one of: ADMIN, ARBITRARY, or GROUP.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.

dev:type.dev:name: None. You cannot pipe objects to this command.
Returns a hashtable containing the recipient configuration.

maml:introduction.maml:para: ``` New-LMRecipient -Type ADMIN -Addr "admin@company.com" -Method "email" Creates a new admin recipient that will receive email notifications. ```
``` New-LMRecipient -Type GROUP -Addr "EmergencyContacts" Creates a new recipient that references an existing recipient group. ```

The New-LMRecipientGroup function creates a new LogicMonitor recipient group with the specified parameters.

The description of the recipient group.
The name of the recipient group. This parameter is mandatory.
A object containing the recipients for the recipient group. The object must contain a "method", "type" and "addr" key.
